title: Revert build_trial_values to direct-insert loop (hot path allocation regression)
analysis: Task-589 refactored build_trial_values (solver.rs:75) to call params_to_value_map, which allocates an intermediate HashMap<ValueCellId, Value> that is immediately iterated over to insert into the PersistentMap, then dropped. This is called inside ConstraintCostFunction::cost() on every Nelder-Mead iteration (up to 5000 per solve). The fix is surgical: inline the param→Value::Scalar mapping directly into build_trial_values' insert loop, eliminating the intermediate HashMap allocation. params_to_value_map remains for the three cold-path call sites (early-exit at line 517, fallback at line 625, solution construction at line 657). The existing test build_trial_values_inserts_auto_params (line 684) covers single-param behavior. We add a multi-param regression test and an empty-params edge-case test before making the change, then refactor.

steps.description: Write a multi-param regression test for build_trial_values that exercises multiple params of different types (length + angle) and verifies each is inserted with the correct si_value and dimension, while preserving non-auto base values. This locks in behavior before the refactor.
Write an edge-case test for build_trial_values with empty params slice, verifying the base ValueMap is returned unchanged (no insertions, no panics).

Refactor build_trial_values to use a direct-insert loop: replace the `for (id, value) in params_to_value_map(params, x)` with `for (param, &val) in params.iter().zip(x.iter())` that directly constructs Value::Scalar { si_value: val, dimension: dimension_of(&param.param_type) } and inserts param.id.clone() into the ValueMap. This eliminates the intermediate HashMap allocation on the hot path.

Update the doc-comment on build_trial_values to reflect it no longer calls params_to_value_map, and verify all existing tests pass (build_trial_values_inserts_auto_params, params_to_value_map_builds_correct_hashmap, plus the two new tests from steps 1-2, plus full solver integration tests).

design_decisions.decision: Inline the param-to-Value::Scalar mapping rather than extracting a shared iterator helper
design_decisions.rationale: The two usages have different collection targets (PersistentMap vs HashMap), so a shared iterator adapter would add abstraction without simplifying either call site. Direct code in each function is clearer and avoids lifetime/type complexity.

design_decisions.decision: Keep params_to_value_map unchanged for cold-path call sites
design_decisions.rationale: The three cold-path call sites (lines 517, 625, 657) need a HashMap<ValueCellId, Value> for SolveResult::Solved { values }. params_to_value_map serves them correctly and the allocation is immaterial since each runs at most once per solve.

design_decisions.decision: Use dimension_of(&param.param_type) inline instead of duplicating the match
design_decisions.rationale: dimension_of is already a small helper that handles the Type → DimensionVector mapping. Calling it from both build_trial_values and params_to_value_map avoids any logic duplication.

reuse.how: Called in the new direct-insert loop to get the DimensionVector for each param, same as params_to_value_map does internally
Serves as primary regression test for single-param behavior — must continue to pass after refactor
Verifies params_to_value_map is unmodified — must continue to pass since cold-path call sites still use it

reuse.what: PersistentMap O(1) clone + O(log n) insert
reuse.how: build_trial_values leverages structural sharing — base.clone() is O(1), each insert is O(log n), total O(k log n) for k auto params
